goals.text: Ship v1 of the Ledger billing API for internal consumers in 8 weeks.
p95 read-path latency under 120ms; p95 write-path under 250ms.
One stable error envelope across every endpoint — no raw stack traces ever reach a client.
OpenAPI spec generated from the same type definitions the runtime uses.

audience.text: Internal services within the Acme platform (3 consumers today, 6 planned by v1).
Consumer SDK authors who will codegen typed clients from the OpenAPI spec.
Oncall engineers who need structured logs that answer "what went wrong for request X."

constraints.text: Node 20 + Fastify 4 + PostgreSQL 16 (managed). No ORM for v1 — typed query layer over pg.
Auth is API-key only for v1 (internal service mesh). OAuth 2.1 client-credentials is a v2 follow-up.
Deployment target: containers on the shared Acme platform (not serverless).
Observability stack is fixed: structured JSON logs → Loki; traces → Tempo; metrics → Prometheus.

assumptions.text: Traffic stays under 500 rps during the alpha window; headroom is fine.
Internal consumers accept alpha-level SLAs (99.5% availability, 48h incident-response).
No public-internet exposure in v1 — mTLS at the mesh layer handles transport security.

risks.text: Downstream dependency on the legacy Pricing service (SLA not formally documented).
Schema drift between services during migration from the existing monolith — need a contract-check step.
Pager fatigue from over-alerting on structured log volume; need explicit log-level discipline.

repoConventions.text: TypeScript strict mode is on. Never use `any`; prefer `unknown` + narrow at the boundary.

uxRules.text: Error codes are stable strings ("auth.invalid_token", "ratelimit.exceeded"), not HTTP numbers. Clients switch on them.
Pagination uses cursor tokens, not offset+limit — drop offset from the public surface entirely.
Timestamps are ISO-8601 UTC strings. No unix epochs, no timezone guessing.

a11yRules.text: All interactive elements must be reachable and operable with the keyboard alone.
Focus must be visible at all times; never remove outlines without replacing them.
Text contrast must meet WCAG AA (4.5:1 for body, 3:1 for large text).
Every form control has a programmatic label.
Pages use proper landmark regions (header, main, nav, footer) and a sensible heading order.
Responses include explicit Content-Language when returning localised error messages.

componentGuidance.text: ErrorEnvelope — one JSON shape for every 4xx/5xx: { error: { code, message, requestId, details? } }.
RequestContext — per-request { requestId, traceId, user?, origin } carried through the handler stack.
RouteHandler — typed (req, ctx) => Promise<Response>. Zod-parses input, narrows at the edge, never leaks internal fields.
RateLimiter — middleware keyed on (user || ip, route). Returns 429 with Retry-After when tripped.
Logger — structured JSON only. One line per request (method, path, status, duration_ms, requestId, userId).

qaGuardrails.text: Unit tests live next to source as `*.test.ts`. Run `vitest run` in CI and before every commit touching business logic.
Success criterion to verify before launch: Every endpoint documented in the generated OpenAPI spec with examples + error-envelope shape.
Success criterion to verify before launch: Integration tests cover happy path, 4xx auth, 4xx validation, 5xx upstream-down — per endpoint.
Success criterion to verify before launch: p95 latency budgets met under synthetic load for both read and write paths.
Success criterion to verify before launch: Zero raw stack traces in client responses across 7 days of alpha traffic.
Success criterion to verify before launch: One-line log per request including requestId, userId, route, status, duration_ms.
Every 4xx / 5xx response conforms to the ErrorEnvelope shape; no raw framework errors leak.
Every handler has a typed input schema (zod or equivalent); invalid payloads get 400 with field-level details.
/health and /ready are always reachable, never gated by auth, and respond in under 50ms.
No secrets in logs, even at debug level. Redaction applied before the log line is emitted.
p95 latency budget declared per route and monitored; SLOs published.
Integration tests hit a real database / cache (or ephemeral container), not mocks.

dosAndDonts.text: Prefer small, focused diffs. One concern per PR or patch.
Always show the exact test or typecheck command you ran and its result.
When following a rule from this file, cite it by section heading so reviewers can audit.
Do not refactor code unrelated to the task, even if it looks better.
Do not add new dependencies without flagging the choice and reason.
Add new routes under /v{N}/ — versioning is the only breaking-change escape hatch.
Validate inputs with zod schemas at the handler boundary; keep the parsed type flowing through the rest of the request.
Propagate requestId into every log line and downstream call (X-Request-Id header).
Do not add endpoints without a typed input schema and an ErrorEnvelope-shaped error path.
Do not introduce an ORM without approval — start with a typed query layer, add ORM only if read/write volume demands it.
Do not return 200 with `{ error: ... }` payloads. HTTP status is the source of truth for success vs failure.

unsafeActions.text: Never modify generated files (anything under `dist/`, `build/`, `.next/`, or marked `@generated`).
Never commit secrets. Redact any `.env*` content that appears in logs or diffs.
Never log full request or response bodies at info level. Redacted summaries only.
Never expose stack traces or ORM error messages to clients. Map to the ErrorEnvelope.

responseExpectations.text: Before claiming work is complete, run the repo's typecheck and test commands and show the output.
If the brief is ambiguous on a specific section, ask rather than guess. Cite the section heading.

routes.text: /ready — readiness probe. Fails if downstream deps (db, cache, upstream API) are unhealthy.
/metrics — Prometheus text exposition. Restricted by network or bearer token.
/v1/* — versioned public surface. Breaking changes move to /v2/, never rename in place.

components.text: Feature components live co-located with the route or feature folder that owns them.

sprint1.text: Commit the Project Spine–generated `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, and `.github/copilot-instructions.md` after review.
Pick and configure a linter (Biome or ESLint) with a minimal rule set.
Add a minimal CI workflow that runs typecheck + lint + tests on every PR.
Deliver: Ship v1 of the Ledger billing API for internal consumers in 8 weeks.
Deliver: p95 read-path latency under 120ms; p95 write-path under 250ms.
Deliver: One stable error envelope across every endpoint — no raw stack traces ever reach a client.
Deliver: OpenAPI spec generated from the same type definitions the runtime uses.

warnings.message: Framework detection confidence 0.4. Evidence: no framework dep detected
warnings.suggestion: Install the framework dependency explicitly (e.g., `npm i next`), or bootstrap from a framework starter before compiling.
Run `spine compile --brief ./brief.md --repo .` to generate AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and copilot-instructions.md.
